question: Develop a C++ program that converts a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit. The program should ask the user for the temperature in Celsius and display the equivalent in Fahrenheit. (Formula: F = C * 9/5 + 32)

test_cases.expected: Enter temperature in Celsius: Temperature in Fahrenheit: 77.00
Enter temperature in Celsius: Temperature in Fahrenheit: 32.00
Enter temperature in Celsius: Temperature in Fahrenheit: 50.00

errors: The program calculates Fahrenheit using floating-point types (double) but then explicitly casts the result to an integer (int) before displaying it. This semantic error causes a loss of precision, as any decimal part of the Fahrenheit temperature is truncated, contrary to the typical expectation of displaying equivalent temperatures with appropriate precision (e.g., 77 instead of 77.00).
